(sha=54a4e2f50ea80ba7a9177ac56cb9fe4c7964dc77)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a transcript-reading helper and wires agent_end, before_compaction, and before_reset hook payloads to use JSONL transcript messages so provenance fields survive.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: sessions_send creates inputProvenance, persistence writes it to JSONL user messages, and current agent_end still emits the in-memory messagesSnapshot that may not carry transcript-only fields.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer review for compaction conversation-access policy and a dirty rebase; this is not a safe automated repair lane yet.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- Manual resets, idle resets, and reset preservation behavior are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured from the patched branch:
  ```text
  $ git branch --show-current
  fix-daily-session-reset

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed
  Tests       15 passed

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-close.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed
  Tests       40 passed
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The scheduler coverage resets a stale daily session during the maintenance sweep and leaves a fresh post-boundary session unchanged.
- What was not tested: A long-running gateway naturally crossing the configured reset hour.


## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenario: a stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message, while a fresh daily session is left alone.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh daily sessions remain unchanged.

### Actual

The focused scheduler tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused test commands and passing results are listed above under real behavior proof.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
target.author: stevenepalmer
